mnemonics.title: ACID Properties
mnemonics.content: Remember ACID as 'A Transaction is Completely Indivisible and Durable' - A(Atomicity): All or Nothing, C(Consistency): Valid State to Valid State, I(Isolation): No Interference, D(Durability): Saved Permanently

acronyms.usage: Four fundamental properties that ensure reliable transaction processing in databases
Notation for representing concurrent transactions in schedules

visualTips.visualization: Draw a circle with arrows: Active → Partially Committed → Committed (success path), Active → Failed → Aborted (failure path), Aborted can restart transaction

Think of a bank transfer: Atomicity (entire transfer or nothing), Consistency (balance must be correct before and after), Isolation (other transactions don't see intermediate state), Durability (once completed, it's permanent in records)
Use precedence graph - draw nodes for transactions, arrows for conflicting operations. If no cycles, schedule is serializable

analogies.analogy: Like a flight booking - either all steps (payment, seat allocation, confirmation) complete or the booking is cancelled entirely, no partial bookings
Like a balance sheet - transactions must follow accounting rules, invalid ones are rejected like bouncing a bad check
Like two people using a shared document with edit locking - they take turns or see consistent snapshots, not half-finished edits from each other
Like a saved game file - once you save (commit), your progress persists even if the console turns off
